anAzure Resource Manager template(ARM

to using thedenyeffect as it lets resources continue to be created,
but ensures the changes are made to make them compliant.

object, you can create a single assignment that would have been multiple assignments.

title: Pattern: Logical operators in a policy definition - Azure Policy
display_title: Use logical operators in Azure Policy definitions
description: This Azure Policy pattern provides examples of how to use the logical operators in a policy definition.
summary:
A policy definition can contain several conditional statements. You might need each statement to be
true or only need some of them to be true. To support these needs, the language haslogical operatorsfornot,allOf,
andanyOf. They're optional and can be nested to create complex scenarios.
classification_reason: Pattern page with concrete JSON policy snippets showing not, allOf, anyOf usage in Azure Policy. These are product-specific policy language patterns and structures, fitting integrations/coding patterns more than generic concepts.

title: Pattern: Parameters in a policy definition - Azure Policy
display_title: Define and use parameters in Azure Policy definitions

by usingparameters. The parameter is defined
during policy assignment. Parameters have a set of pre-defined properties that describe the
parameter and how it's used.

title: Tutorial: Build policies to enforce compliance - Azure Policy
description: In this tutorial, you use policies to enforce standards, control costs, maintain security, and impose enterprise-wide design principles.
summary:
Understanding how to create and manage policies in Azure is important for staying compliant with
your corporate standards and service-level agreements. In this tutorial, you learn to use Azure
Policy to do some of the more common tasks related to creating, assigning, and managing policies
